title: A Practical Checklist for Verifying AI Answers Against Your Data
summary: Before you paste an AI summary into a slide, run this checklist against the same file the AI used.

content.summary: Before you paste an AI summary into a slide, run this checklist against the same file the AI used.

body.text: Executives remember the number on the chart, not the chat transcript. Verification connects both before anyone acts. Start with row count after filters: preview should match what you intended. Build a chart for the metric the AI stated—total, average, top category—and compare digits.

body.alt: Professional reviewing business charts on a laptop
body.caption: Professional reviewing business charts on a laptop. Photo: Unsplash

body.text: Start with row count after filters: preview should match what you intended. Build a chart for the metric the AI stated—total, average, top category—and compare digits.
If chart and AI disagree, inspect column types and null handling. Text-formatted amounts and blank rows are the usual suspects.

items: Apply saved filter rules before asking and before charting.
Screenshot chart and AI response together for audit trails.
Escalate recurring mismatches to column naming standards.

body.text: Do not retry with vaguer questions. Tighten column references and date ranges until a simple total matches.
Teach teams that unverified AI output is draft text, not a metric—culture beats any software setting.
Datilio makes verification natural: Talk to Data sits beside preview and charts on one upload.

meta_description: Before you paste an AI summary into a slide, run this checklist against the same file the AI used.
meta_keywords: Verification, AI Trust, Checklist, Data Quality
